conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
That helps, knowing you're feeling a bit more at ease already. So, what brought you to this appointment today?
Honestly, just needed someone to talk to, I guess. Work's been kind of tough lately.
Can you tell me a bit more about that? What specifically is making it challenging for you?
Just feeling guilty about some mistakes I made. Hard to sleep at night, thinking about them.
Guilt can be really debilitating. Have you noticed any changes in your daily routine or schedule because of this guilt?
Yeah, I've been getting up later and later just to avoid dealing with it all.
And how's that working out for you? Do you feel like you're able to cope with things more effectively when you're not facing the day as early?
Honestly, no. It's just made everything worse, I think.
Okay, let's explore this guilt a bit further. What are some specific mistakes that are keeping you up at night?
Just some decisions I made that affected the team's performance, and now they're all on me to fix it.
I see. And how do you think you could approach fixing those issues in a way that would alleviate some of this guilt for you?
Honestly, not sure yet. Still trying to figure it out.
That's okay! We can work through this together. Can you tell me more about your role at the store? What do you enjoy most about it?
Um, I mean, just the sense of control and responsibility, I guess. Trying to keep everyone happy and productive.
That sounds like a lot of pressure! Do you feel supported by your colleagues or management in dealing with those responsibilities?
Eh, they're trying, but sometimes it feels like no one really gets me.
It can be tough when we feel like we're not being fully understood. Have you talked to anyone about this at work? Any friends or family members you've opened up to?
Just my mom, I guess. She's always been supportive, but it's hard to talk about work stuff with her.
It can be helpful to have someone outside of the situation to share your concerns with. Would that be something you'd like to explore further?
Yeah, maybe. We'll see.
Okay, we'll definitely keep that in mind as we move forward. What do you hope to get out of our sessions together? Any specific goals or areas you'd like to work on?
Just getting my head clear and figuring out how to make some changes, I suppose.
That's a great starting point! We can definitely work towards those goals. Before we wrap up today, is there anything that's been on your mind lately that you haven't had a chance to talk about yet?
Just the whole feeling of being stuck and not knowing how to get out of it.
I hear you. Feeling stuck can be really frustrating. Let's make sure to keep an eye on that as we move forward, okay?
Yeah, okay.
One more thing before we wrap up – what are your biggest concerns or fears right now? Any worries that are keeping you up at night?
Just the fear of not being able to turn things around and being stuck in this rut forever.
I understand. It sounds like there's a lot on your plate, but we can work together to address those concerns and help you feel more in control. Let's schedule another session for next week, okay?
Yeah, that sounds good.
Great! We'll take things one step at a time, and I'm looking forward to seeing how you progress. Thank you for sharing this with me today.
Thanks.
You're welcome. Take care of yourself until next week, okay?
Yeah, will do.
